name: Dispersed Camping close to Canyonlands NP Needles District
description: Just 5 miles outside the Park. Nice Panorama, but close to HW 211 (quiet at night). Free. No facilities.

name: Utah Welcome Center
description: Rest Area on 70W before Thompson Springs. Very peaceful night. Several trucks and overnighters here as well. Clean bathrooms. Water spigot appears to be OFF.

name: Horseshoe Overlook Road
description: Parking lot. Accessible with a European RV.

name: Irish Canyon
description: Nice area for free camping near the Glen Canyon NRA. Some spots have fire pits. Camping officially allowed. FREE

name: Crystal Geyser
description: Free. Unestablished sites. We didnt see the geyser go off. Apparently its one or two times a day. A good overnight spot. 10 km off the highway on unmaintained washboard potholed road. One bar of Verizon. Permit required for river trips.

name: Lone Mesa Campground
description:
Only self-contained campers. Portable Toilet required

There are many many places for dispersed camping along Dubinky Wells Road based on recommendation of Canyonlands ranger. 
There are no amenities here but it's good for when the official campsites are full. Only takes abou…

name: Outside Arches
description: Inside Arches you pay 25 dollar, around arches you pay 15 dollar. On this spot you pay nothing and also got the amazing view. Follow the road and go on the dirtroad (at the beginning you see a sign of a trail). When you are up on the platform you have a great view and nobody can…

name: La Sal Forest
description: Camping free Lá Sal Forest. It's fresh, good to relax and sleep. Moab is very hot in summer. In midle of trees, lots of shadow. No amenities but place to fire. Near cold river and near a great lake with pic nic area. Very good here.

name: Uncompahgre Nat. Forest
description:
Schöne Plätze auf der Mesa

Domingues-Escalante Forest. Access marked on Hwy 141 on the left. Up on the Mesa, a few flat campsites with fire rings near the top. Gravel road in good condition, good for car and suv's, just watch for atv's and pickup trucks tumbling down as if it…

name: Uncompahgre Nat Forest
description:
Many beautiful places on the Mesa


Viele schöne Plätze auf der Mesa

name: Beef Basin Road - Top
description: Very nice place with a fantastic view. Beetwen trees with firering.

name: Maverick Gas Station
description: Potable water on the left side of the building near pump 11. This is a FREE dump station at the Maverick Gas Station. Open 24h for water potable and dump station. Gas cheap.

name: Turnout before Needles overlook
description: Quiet and secluded place on a trail 2.2 km on the left of the road to Needles.Dispersed camping, no amenities. High on the plateau, can be very cold at night.

name: Kokopelli Trail
description: The Kokopelli trail is marked, turnoff from hwy 128 north of Moab toward I-70 past the 30-mile no-camping area. 4x4 only, trailhead is relatively smooth at first, then very rough. You can camp anywhere. This area is quite different from the Moab-Arches area, more desert-like, no…

name: Out of Canyon Lands
description: Road is easy to get to. Dry camping. nice. no trash. this will be are 3rd night. stars are awesome. free camping

name: Salt Valley Road/ BLM 145
description: Overnight place near to Arches NP. There are dozens of possibilities on this road. Ranger says anywhere in BLM that you can find a safe pull off is OK to stay at. After dark, no traffic at all. Just be sure to bring water and leave no trace. And enjoy the stars. :)

name: Island in the Sky Road BLM
description: Nice spot behind rocks. Near to Island in the Sky Canyonlands NP. Dirty road to get there. 4-wheele drive and high clearance needed do not attempt with a travel trailer or RV as the road is horrible and no way to turn around.

name: Hart Point Road
description: There are several overnight spots on that road. Can be windy on the hills. This site has a fire pit and space for 2-3 cars. Near to Needles Canyonlands NP.

name: Blackridge Road Colorado
description: A small place next to the radio tower entrance with a great view over the Colorado National Monument Area.

name: La Sal Mountains - nice view
description: Parking lot trailhead - with a great view over the Mesa. There are other possibilities in the forest around, especially during summer.

name: Beef Basin Road - Top View
description: A nice Place with a firering and a really beautiful view over the mountains

name: BLM land near Canyonlands NP
description: Stayed 2 nights here without issues. It does get windy so our doors were kept shut. On the second night, we tried to go a bit further down the road on a 2WD promaster, oh booooy that was an adventure!! Next place to turnaround was past some very rough terrain, we managed to surv…

name: Gemini Bridges
description: Grand spot! This established free BLM campground has 5 sites clustered together at the bottom of a really beautiful red rock canyon, but there is a lot of privacy and you can't really see the other campers, beside most of the time there is nobody around. The campsites are flat w…

name: Gearheads
description: Outdoor gear store offering free filtered water inside the store. Bring and fill your own container. A cart is provided so you can wheel your jugs back to your vehicle.

name: Near the monticello lake
description:
Nice place close to the lake. Very quiet, good temperature when it's too hot in Moab and canyonsland !! Good tires need, high clearance. 
Toilets. FREE

name: Valley City Road
description: Very quiet. No Services. Good sunrise. Love it

name: Athena Launch Complex
description:
If you ever wanted to camp beside an abandoned nuclear missile silo, here is your chance. Still can't believe this.

Several level spots right beside or on the concrete launch pads or infront of the control bunker. 
Free, very quiet, good vistas. Gates on all sides are open, see…

name: Rock Valley
description: Half a mile off the highway behind a hill, there are a bunch of flat spots with fire pits good for bigger rigs and RVs. If you don't mind driving down a bumpy dirt track and got high clearance there are a couple more nice secluded spots surrounded by boulders near a seasonal cre…

name: Harley's Dome View Area
description:
Parking lot (not leveled everywhere) on I-70 westbound 1/2 mile prior to exit 227 Westwater. 
Short steep trail leading to outlook with great view of open plains and mountains. Good AT&T signal. Good Tmobile.

name: Free camping in beautyfull canyon, next Canyonland N.P. south
description:
Follow hwy 211, aprox. 5 mile before you enter the N.P. go north on Hamburger Rd. Pass the BLM-Campground to your right (also nice but 10usd) and continue on that gravel road into the canyon. 
You can camp on many sites but if you cross the "indian creek" (just 4x4) there is a n…

name: Across Hite, right at Colorado River
description:
There is a gravel road down to the plateau on the river. no sign, we had no problems.
other local camper stayed there too. great views, quiet at night!

name: Dubinky Well Rd #1
description: Dispersed camping off of Dubinky Well Rd. There is a large “cul-de-sac” here with a fire pit in the middle. The spot is large enough for multiple rigs. This location as a few trees, which we camped in between. There are no signs that prohibit camping here and it got quite busy o…

name: Moab - Lions Park Spigot
description: Two threaded spigots in the lower lot- there is also a filtered bottle fill station in the pavilion up the stairs. There are electric outlets in the pavilion Carrie the grass if you need to charger computer/jackery and tables to sit down on in the shade

name: A mighty wind
description: Beautiful stop on the bluffs over looking Fruita. Just outside Colorado National Monument. Drive through the park to get to the rattlesnake canyon access road. Can get reallly windy

name: BLM camping-Lone Mesa Campground
description: This is BLM camping 7 miles down Lone Mesa Campground Rd. A few sites along designated road. 4x4/High Clearance recommended.

name: La Sal Loop offshoot
description: shortly down a double track road off the main paved road, there is a dead end with a very big view that would probably be a nice place to camp. Anytime outside of summer/fall it may be quite cold at night or inaccessible due to snow, but otherwise a great way to get out of the M…

name: National Forest near Monticello
description: A grassy spot in an oak grove. Little bumpy. Rock fire ring. No facilities. We slept here one night without any problem. It would fit more cars/campers.

name: Manti-La Sal National Forest
description: All the way up the hill there are small side roads and pull ins. All of them were full with campers as i rode up on a Saturday. Managed to find this one amongst the trees and bushes. Flat soft ground, great for tents. Just a few rock fire pits around.

name: Near Needles Overlook. Before 'No Camping' Area
description:
Most or all of the camping immediately before Needles Overlook has been closed as of May 2018. 

This camping site's turnoff is just before the no camping sign. There is a moderately sized flat area on the right side of the paved road opposite the two track to the South. Take …

name: BLM north of Canyonlands National Park
description:
No Camping Allowed Past this point sign

Bunch grass, sage and Pinon Pine, a few cows with new spring calves. Feels almost like home (Wyoming), which is a good thing. Just off one of the access roads to the White Rim Trail.

name: Manti La Sal National Forest
description: Quiet, nice and level place, surrounded by trees and rocks. Two spots with fireplace.

name: Grand View Point Road/Island in the Sky BLM
description: Found a nice little turn off with nice level ground off the dirt road that heads south from Grand View Point Rd/Island in the Sky road. No facilities but if you have a 4x4 and don't mind boondocking this place is a must! Was able to get a 200kb/s LTE signal from the T-Mobile tow…

name: LaSal Mts Overlook
description: At marker 4661 on the left side there are 10 or more wild campsites on BLM/USFS lands with firepits. They all have a stunning overlook of Moab and the canyons below. Its much cooler and secluded in the mountains than other sites. When I was there only one night out of three did …

name: BLM land
description: Just go through gate in cattle fence and shut it behind you. Camp anywhere if you are self contained. Very quiet with good night sky. It is free and no one will bother you. I car camped close to the road behind a rise, but you can go a couple miles to the west toward a creek and…

name: Colorado River Rimming near Hite
description: Pull off road on eastern side of bridge to the left if travelling east. Hide car behind the rock wall. We camped a level down from road outside of view. rock fire pit no shade but amaaazing views. can scramble down to the river a little upstream from bridge. some bridge/road noi…

name: end of the road
description:
Large stony clearing. Deer, wild turkey cows.
For those with a tent a grass clearing with a flat space is just above.

name: green river Amtrak station
description: Amtrak parking lot. next to train track. stayed 1 night and no one bothered us.

name: At the Cowground with nice sunset view
description: Not far from the road but very nice sunset view. Not if is wet, then the road becomes muddy. 2 mi before sign "no camping"

name: La Sal Mountain Loop Road, Manti-La Sal NF
description: Great Campsite in Manti-La Sal National Forest with an amazing view to the La Sal Mountains to the West and Arches National Park, Moab Rim, San Rafael Swell and Henry Mountains to the East. The Dirtroad is bumpy but ok with 2 WD.

name: Mineral Bottom Road, Canyonlands
description: Great Campsite on Slickrock at Horsethief Spring Track branching off from Mineral Bottom Road with an wide view across Horsethief Canyonand the Island in the Sky north of Canyonlands National Park.

name: Beef Basin Road
description: Great Campsite along Beef Basin Road at the foot of Bridger Jack Mesa with nice view to the Abajo Mountains.

name: Beef Basin Road
description: Dispersed camping along Beef Basin Road in Indian Creek National Recreation Area. The road and area is very muddy when wet.

name: 24/7 Moab Laundry Express with WIFI and toilets
description: Seems to be the only laundromat (24/7) still in service in town. 3.50usd for small loads, 4.50usd for middle and 6usd for large ones. Typical dryers with quarters. Free fast wifi & toilets inside for customers only.

name: BLM Moab
description: Easy access, plenty of space, some highway noise, free, 15-20 minutes from Moab. BLM so no services. Spotty cell service.

name: Angels Cove trailhead
description:
Great spot . Trail leading down to the Dirty Devil river. OK to camp here told us the BLM ranger.

There are two roads leading here from highway 95. The better one is the southern: It starts about 10.5 miles south from Hanksville at a large gravel parking lot. The orher road f…

name: Suds & Spin (formerly Fruita laundromat)
description: Most of the backing you need to buy a card to use in them. There are some coin operated machines. 2.25 to wash. .75/8 min to dry. Nice clean but small laundromat with washroom

name: BLM camping just off Needles Overlook Road
description: Red dirt road through cow grazing land. No fire rings. We just parked off the dirt road. Beautiful mountain views. Super quiet. Pup enjoyed running around!

name: Exxon
description:
Potable water available at the gas station - bring your own hose. Easy to fill jugs without hose. 

Spigot located under tree on south side of parking lot.

name: National Forest parking area
description: Large gravel parking area. The road further into the forest here is only open May 15 through Nov.

name: Mineral Bottom BLM
description: Fantastic place by the mineral canyon. Lots of options to camp by the road before here, or here with an amazing view. Road in good conditions when it’s dry. This was a nice tip from a local guide. No facilities. No wood, no water.

name: Quiet spot on cliff over Indian Creek
description: Stunning beautiful and quiet spot 3.5 miles down Lockhart Basin Rd. First 2 miles groomed gravel, then it’s a combination or sand, rock, and a crossing of Indian creek. We did it in the spring and there was about 6 inches of swiftly moving water but the bottom was flat and not s…

name: OK Anderson Park
description:
MODERATOR NOTE: Seasonal Closures reported.

Water available from tap near the picnic tables covered area. Would need a 10 mete hose

name: Switchback on FS 093
description: Just as you begin to transition from trees to Desert (or desert to trees if going uphill) on FS 093, there is a nice flat section of slick rock sandwiched in the switchback. With fire ring and 2 Verizon bars. 4WD only, and even then, if it rains, you might want chains. Good view.

name: Athena Outpost
description: Beautiful views. Thin unmaintained road leading to this spot. It’s a gravel road, so made it easy to get to. I wouldn’t recommend trailers, the turn around spot at top does not seem big enough. Built in breakfast table though. About 8 suvs went to the Athena Launch Pad in the mo…

name: Fruita bike field
description: You can take a right at the beginning of the dirt road that leads to the camping and camp in the fields. Quiet and wonderful sunset and sun rise Many RVs & campers were here as well

name: Westwater
description: Primitive camping spot on a ridge. Good sunset, space for multiple rigs. Have seen a 30’ camper make it in from the west end of road. West entry starts south of airport/runway(5+ spots lower). Take a left and go straight past signage. Follow up hill. East entry is 4x4 approach o…

name: Sierra Petroleum
description: Dirt lot next to highway (quiet at night) flat-ish ok for overnight parking.

name: Canyon view Forest
description: Awesome view from top of the mountains down into the canyon. Road needs 4x4. Absolut quiet and pure wilderness here... ;-). John Brown Road Close. Did it with motorbike. #freiheitenwelt

name: Maverick gas station parking
description: Next to the dump there is a truck parking, clean and for big Rv. At the shop I was told to park for overnight near the white fence. No truck idling, but generators may be used during the night.

name: Off dirt road in National Forest
description: Dirt road off main highway labeled National Forest access. A number of sites along road for camping.

name: BLM area on cliff edge with mountain view
description: 3.3 miles from highway, dirt road which will shake you up but quite doable. Turn off into very short rough track with washouts so high clearance required. There are a series of flat campsite right on the edge of the cliff facing a snow covered mountain. One other vehicle there w…

name: Mineral Rd creekbed
description:
This spot is approximately 3.5km down Mineral Creek on the left (North) side of the road as you travel East.

4x4 high clearance required. You will travel over a dry creekbed with some larger rocks.

The spot I camped at is a little bit lower than the road so can be easy to …

name: Leprechaun Trailhead
description: Around 4 to 5 spots at the Trailhead to Leprechaun Canyon. There are a few more behind the little sandy hill to the left, but there's soft sand behind and you need 4 x 4 in this area. no service at all.

name: Green River, West Bank
description: Pitched up on west bank by bridge on short dirt track near Pilot Travel Center. Minimal traffic, swam in river, secluded and safe

name: Salt Valley
description: Near the back entrance/exit of Arches off of Salt Valley Rd. Take the two-track north east towards the rock face. Flat ground for tent or car/camping. Bring your gear if you're a climber and fire ring is next to some beautiful stone. Not much for shade. Quiet. Off the beaten pat…

name: Blue Hills Rd.
description: BLM, 20 min drive up Hwy 191 from Moab. Right next to the airport. Just drive up the road and into the field, you will probably see campers.

name: Isle in the Sky BLM
description: The end of the road. Drive in is two track through lots of SOFT sad. Beware if rain is forecasted, you may not get out. This place offers a stunning view of canyons and major rock formations that I would assume hold some good climbing so bring those ropes! A soft decent down off…

name: La sal loop Rd
description: 15 mins from Castle creek. Several fire rings in a wooded setting. Three bars of Verizon.

name: San Miguel river camp free
description: look for the tracks leading into the bush from CO141 follow to large camp area with fire pit great views some road noise! free 7 day camp!

name: Airport C&D
description:
This site requires a Backcountry Permit from the Canyonlands National Park office.

Camp Site with large spaces along the White Rim Trail

name: Airport A & B
description:
This site requires a Backcountry Permit from the Canyonlands National Park office.

Campsite along White Rim trail. pit bathroom set

name: Mineral Canyon
description: Spectacular drive down IN the canyon. Sandy road. At Green River. Pass the boat ramp. A lot of Trees for shade. Flat. Accessible also for 2WD when dry. Fire pit. Amazing view. Totally alone! One of these spots

name: Arches NP / Salt Valley Wash
description: Flat. At Arches Salt Wash (Salt Valley Road). Spectacular sunset. Really at backdoor Arches NP. You sleep next to the northern (less used) entrance of the parc. Very beautiful scenery. Almost no cars passing. Possible with 2WD when dry. Gravel and Sandy road.

name: Exxon
description:
There is a red water tap available behind the smaller white brick building next to the gas station (where the big trucks are parked).

I didn't ask if I could use it. Honestly I just filled up my tank and went on my way. No problems. All water here is potable.

name: FR4643 off LaSal Loop road above Moab
description:
About 2 miles down 4643 west toward Moab. 

4 wheel drive not needed going in (down) if wet you’ll need it getting in or out.

4643 links to Sandy Flats Road at the bottom. So you can just keep going down the hill.

Ground clearance a must. Better have 6-8 inches minimum i…

name: Gemini Bridge Road
description: Wild camp at the entrance of the road. Excellent place to spend a night. A good place when campgrounds are full. Variable service on At&t and Mint Mobile.

name: BLM land past Lone Mesa Campground
description:
PORTABLE TOILET REQUIRED AND ENFORCED

Gorgeous BLM land past Lone Mesa Group campground and just before Lona Mesa Campground on BLM ROAD 137. 4wd and high clearance recommended, but not necessary. I’ve seen freightliners and sprinters with low clearance on the road. There are…

name: Herbie's Cattle Gate Park
description: Great view. Plenty of room for any type vehicle. Several fire rings. Great hiking spot there too. Spotty cell service.

name: Fallen Peace Officer Trailhead
description:
Parking next to the road but quiet at night. Only us here, while the spot at Dalton Wells Road was crowded. At the parking lot further, it's written 'no camping', but not here at the entrance.
We are a 35ft motorhome with a trailer. We didn't have any trouble finding level grou…

name: Rim Rocker Mesa
description: Free beautiful overlook. Primative, on the middle section of the Rim Rocker trail. Great views and abundent firewood.

name: Blm road 226
description:
*Edit: can't access as of April 2026 without a high clearance 4x4 and a lot of determination.*

Fire pit And large flat area a couple miles in a dirt road but passable for vans etc. beautiful secluded spot with Verizon lte 3 bars

name: Crescent Junction Rest Area
description:
Paved rest area with a nice panoramic lookout. No shade for the summer months. Full bars cell reception. Restrooms and water.

There are lots of truckers that spent the night with us. No signs telling us "No Overnight Camping"

name: Arches Park Gate - BLM
description:
Border BLM and NP (on BLM). 
Beautiful open spot on level rock-surface, BLM land immediately outside the back entrance to Arches National Park. High-clearance essential from the main park road to here (rutts and a rough-rocky surface) 4x4 probably not essential but preferable. …

name: Recreation and Aquatic Center
description:
$12 Adults $6 seniors. 
If you looking for a warm and relatively clean shower, without a time limit, go to the Recreation Aquatic Center here in Moab.
https://moabrecreation.org/216/Amenities


Big hairdryers are on the wall. no hand-dryer.

name: Road 632 just passed Hite
description: Nice turnout on unpaved road that's accessible without a 4x4. There are two spots within 200 feet and both are easy to back in to. Great views and good hiking around the area (no trails). As soon as the sun went down a lot of mosquitoes showed up but otherwise a great spot.

name: Moon Flower Community Coop
description: Nice food coop with free filtered water. The water filling tap is inside store far right back. Very nice coop.

name: Hog Spring Recreation Area
description: Hog Spring Recreation Area. The spot is a pull out with room for a couple of larger RVs along Highway 95. Although it’s along the road at night there is virtually no traffic. It will work for one night.

name: Desert OHV area
description: Easy spot for a night. In the middle of OHV area. Under some power lines and a bit of highway noise. Was quite windy

name: Cliff overlooking Moab&La Sal Mts
description: Beautiful site! 4x4 is absolutely necessary. Cowboy gate at the entrance. The views at this site are some of the best in the US. View of both Moab and Mountains in one sight. Great cell service, fire ring, private. This is true gold

name: Dolores / San Miguel Confluence
description: Nice fire ring, but some road noise. Fantastic vista rock for cocktails, breakfast and ...

name: blm land outskirts of canyonland
description: Off mineral bottom road there are roads that branch off. 4WD recommended and a bit of clearance. We made it with a Tacoma. Set up roof top tent on a slab of rocks, was facing some red rocks. Nice and quiet spot, no fire pit. Only had some curious cows as this is their land. The …

name: Dispersed site with a view
description: Awesome remote site with a pull thru loop. Perfect for a small camper or van.

name: Salt Valley Gorge Road
description: Very large flat lot with epic sunset views. And excellent t-mobile and At&T service!

name: BLM Open Space
description: Nice spot far enough from the Interstate that you don't get much highway noise. Easy for any size rig.

name: Black Dragon Wash BLM
description: Sweet spot right off of the interstate. You literally turn off of the interstate onto a gated dirt road, open the gate, be sure to close it behind you, and follow the roads north for about a mile. It is to the north of the interstate so if coming eastbound you will be turning to…

name: Shadow of Mt. Peale
description:
Really nice spot in a bowl of three major peaks in Manti la Sal. Fire pit is there; spot is pretty flat too. 4x4 recommended. Tree across the road that is doable with high clearance and mud pits. Hopefully parks services clear the tree from the road.

More sights further up th…

name: Fr 4651 LaSal Mountains
description: Large open spaces that are easy to access. bushes add privacy. Decent cellular. Room for any size rig. The road in is Paved. Fr 4651 is open weather permitting. There are 1-5 campsites at this location

name: Beyond the rocks trail site 9 & 10
description: Remote site for small to medium size vehicles

name: Yellow Circle Road
description: We came to explore the other pins and while they would have worked, we really wanted Internet. We made it up the hill with a high clearance van and 4WD was not necessary. It’ll shake you up a bit, but passable. This spot is private, backs up to the canyon, and has a fire ring. T…

name: Buzzards Belly General Store
description: A boondocking spot for overnight stays. Quiet remote spot, clear night skies, great vistas for sunrise and sunset. General store has snacks, drinks and cool stuff. Friendly owner. Van / RV camping allowed but keep close to the road and don’t block access to the store parking. Bu…

name: Indian creek wild camp
description: This is a dirt parking lot I have used before for a overnight with a Sprinter van camp, there is a rock lined campfire ring pretty flat. Off the road about 100' with bushes kind of hiding it.

name: Off road
description:
A small pull out without trees, but no loose sand. Felt safe to pop up eurovan when we arrived after dark. Minimal freeway noise
Beautiful starry night

name: Pilot Travel Center
description: 24 hour travel center, which is nice because you don't have to worry about when to use the restroom. Lots of noise- it's near the ramp to the highway, and cargo trains pass by once in a while. But the sounds are pretty easily blocked out with earplugs. Passenger cars park in fro…

name: School Trust Land
description: Very large area for dispersed camping, only at existing campsites - there are many scattered along the road. Instructions and rules at entrance. Good roads, we are in a 24ft C Class plus 16ft trailer. Amazing views of red rocks and mountains. Free. No amenities. Limited cell sig…

name: BLM Plateau
description: After going down the main road for a while, there is a track that splits off with a nice flat spot with big views of the La Sals. No shade, no wind protection.

name: Tucked away hideout in the canyon
description:
This is a really beautiful spot right off the road (300’ dirt rd) in a very beautiful spot of the canyon, right near hogwash recreation area. 

There is a fire pit in the creek bed as well as near a large rock. Private and tucked away when you park van in back (we have 21’ cla…

name: Laundromat
description: Good Laundromat.You can do laundry here.

name: Beehive Butte View
description: Nice easy spot 1/4 mile off large, well used dirt road. Perfect for groups, trailers, smaller RVs. Take Mineral bottom road 4.1 miles from hwy 313. Just after the right hand curve start looking for a small 4x4 route designation sign on the left with a red sticker on it. make a l…

name: Isolated/LTE (att)
description: Isolated spot for one truck, drive down main road, take a diagonal right on to obvious dirt road, find 2 track on the right drive to end, just enough room to turn around and park. Good ATT LTE signal

name: Mcinnis Conservation parking area
description: We stayed here at the parking area as the other spot on ioverlander near the radio tower required us to drive up Black Ridge road which didn't seem very doable in our transit van (no 4x4). If we went past the Black ridge road GPS was trying to take us on private roads. We walked…

name: Motel 6
description: Large open area at the turn off for Granary trailhead by an old building with "Motel 6" written on it.

name: Love's Gas Station
description: Free to get water and $10 to empty black water. Arby's and gas nearby.

name: Love’s gas station
description: $14 showers, good and clean. towels provided with unlimited time at the Loves Gas Station

name: Off Dubinky Well Road
description:
Open area with a rock fire circle. Little
Higher ground.

name: Steen Road pull offs
description: Beautiful place in the utah desert. A few nice pull offs on a fairly nice dirt road. beware of cows in the road :) Accessable by almost any vehicle. Private Property signs and a fence on the right side of the road for a bit, but most of the road seems friendly to campers. 3 bars…

name: Roberts Bottom Road
description: BLM dispersed site, one of a handful along this road. Beautiful canyon spot with some wind protection, a nice turnaround, stone campfire ring and plenty to explore on foot. Scout around for quartz and agate, if you're into rockhounding.

name: Naturita Sales
description: Water available - ask inside. Also a gas station, café, car wash, convenience store.

name: Laundry
description: effecient laundromat, basic, clean, coin machine on site, bathrooms.

name: Fruita Community Center
description: Showers only for $5. Swimming is $8 Bring your own towels and toiletries. Same entrance as the library.

name: BLM
description: The other spots along this road were taken so when I saw some old tracks going up this little incline I decided to check it out. Nice spot! The ground off the tracks is a little soft so be careful. Panoramic views and excellent T-Mobile 5G! No highway noise and only the occasion…

name: Just Past Hamburger Rock
description: Decent sized and levelish site. Nice views. Easy access off road. The road is very good to this point. We are able to get usable ATT LTE using a very strategically placed Netgear antenna on a Nighthawk portable router.

name: Missile Base Road flats
description: Very large flat area on BLM land. A bit barren, but easy access and 4 bar 5G T-Mobile signal. Small stone fire ring present. Quiet overnight except for very distant I-70 noise.

name: Moab rest stop
description: Rest stop south of Moab. Got into the area after dark and didn’t want to risk the dirt roads in the dark to go to the BLM site I was planning on. Stumbled upon this rest stop. Lots of semis here plus about 8 other vans, travel trailers, and SUVs with people staying the night. Bu…

name: Lazy Lizard Hostel
description:
The Lazy Lizard Hostel offers public showers (from 9am to 9pm) for $4.50/person. Towels for 0.50 cents. You can pay with card, no time limit.

You can lock the shower stall and there is space to put your clothes, etc. No shampoo. Older, however hot and good flow.

Hostel is …

name: Bush Canyon Overlook BLM
description: Decent spot on the edge of some BLM land right off CO-141. Large area just off the road, low clearance & 2wd fine, that could fit several larger rigs. Mostly flat. There are more spots further up on top of the overlook with fire rings, but there are a few big ruts on the way up …

name: Ace Country Coop
description: Water is .40 per gallon. You just have to go in to pay.

name: Boulder Cove Overlook
description: An amazing little spot, right off the road. No need for 4x4, but it really depends on how far down the dirt trail you want to go. There's about 4 to 5 camping spots if you're in a van, or 1 to 2 if you're in a big camper. To get here just take the road off of highway 70, go down…

name: Dominguez-Escalante NCA
description: Opted to stay here over the nearby parking area as we had this little pullout to ourselves. Take 141 to Divide Rd (well graded gravel) and it's about 6 mi, up on top of the mesa. Great views as you drive up, but not many camping options along the narrow road, and none early on. …

name: Harts Draw
description: Great spot on Harts Draw overlooking the mountains. A bar or two of Verizon LTE. Amazing views of the mountains and cliffs. Be careful when turning on the dirt road, there is a noticeable drop.

name: Huge flat lot near highway
description: Huge gravel lot right off the highway. Dirt road continues south from here and likely has more options for sleeping further from the road but I didn’t explore that direction. Good AT&T service. Great sunset views

name: Mineral bottom 360 view
description: Little dirt pull off on the left, empties out into a decent space. It's a little sandy, but probably doable in nearly any vehicle. Road to get here is rough and would recommend going slow, saw a Prius down here, so can't be too bad. Green river nearby for swimming/floating/rafti…

name: Salt valley gorge flats
description: Very large open area. On BLM land, not far from I-70. Fairly flat spot that is suitable for all size rigs and trailers. Road coming from the north has some washboard to it. Not too extreme. Very easy access on and off of the interstate.

name: Coyote
description: Beautiful spot. Fire ring and level. Room for 2 vehicles. Just off spur from Beef Basin Rd. Great 360" views of mountains & canyons. 2+ mi from ruins. No cell.

name: Long Point
description: Small spot with fire ring in ponderosa pine forest. Not far from incredible views of Dark Canyon, Beef Basin and Canyonlands NP.

name: North Fruita Desert BLM spot
description: The spot closer to the freeway was taken so we found another place. Just as nice, really flat, good sunset. Has bugs, but not too many. Stayed weekday night and had total of 3 cars drove by.

name: Four Corners Mine Road
description:
BLM Land off I-70. Pulloff and park close to highway or travel further for more seclusion. A little washboard in areas and some roads may be impassible if there were a flash flood (most land seems prone). 
This is old mine land too so may be unsafe in areas for children or dogs…

name: FR-408 open area w/ LTE (Verizon)
description: Nice site just off Forrest Road 408. Fire ring, and a little bit of wind block. I’m getting 2 to 3 bars of Verizon LTE. Great views, but wide-open and not super-protected...so probably not great for windy nights.

name: Moab Downtown
description: Nice clean hot showers, $6 for non-guest. Park in the back by the General Store and pay inside.

name: La Sal loop Rd
description:
Nice little spot. 2 bars LTE Verizon
Made it with our dually, high roof van, no problem. Fire ring out your right. Pretty leveled. 
The road after this spot gets pretty bad. Don’t recommend unless you have high clearance. Not sure if anymore sites after this one. 
A few spots…

name: Green River Cutoff Road
description:
There is one site at these coordinates, and one on the bluff right above it, near the road. No amenities, just beautiful Utah outdoors. Decent Verizon coverage. 

If you're into rocks, there's seam jasagate down-wash from here. Well, probably everywhere from here, honestly. 
…

name: Just off Needles Overlook Road
description: Just a couple of hundred yards off the main road. slick rock parking area. Nice views of the La Sals.

name: Exxon / 7 Eleven next to Wendy’s
description: Red spigot with hose at Exxon/7-eleven next to Wendy’s. Please make sure and purchase something first and be respectful as the parking lot is small.

name: Moab Park
description: water fountain with hose hookup. Need a long hose to fill a rig, but perfect for Jerry cans (seasonal)

name: BLM Big Rock
description: Big rock with several spots all around it. Several caves to explore. Quiet and flat. Good for small RV. 2 bars of AT&T. Signal is better on the east side of Rock. Just 100-200 meters from dirt road. Some light traffic so go to east side of rock for more solitude. Lots of stars.

name: Off HWY 128 near Moab
description: Beautiful Spot, top of a hill. Nice and easy track to get in, nice views

name: Sinclair’s Gas Station
description: Water spigot on the front of the building. I asked inside if I could use it and the lady had no issue. Must use your own hose.

name: S of Green River, 5mi Athena missile site
description: Abandoned Cold war Athena missile testing site. Old asphalt road is deteriorating but easily possible by automobile. 3-4 bars Verizon.

name: W of Green River 5 miles
description: W of Green River 5 miles. Just off of the interstate at the intersection of Four Corners Mines Road. Nice level wide spots with only minimal interstate traffic noise. 304 bars of Verizon. Easy on and off.

name: BLM off Mineral Creek
description: Awesome rock out crop off a side road from mineral bottom Creek. AWD recommended. a few scrapes on an outback but totally doable

name: BLM Land before the Needles Overlook
description:
BLM land just next top the road of the Needles Overlook (7 min)

The ' No camping beyond this Point' is located 1/4 miles after this campsite, so we suppose it is OK.

There is a flat area, in gravel, where our van can easily access (most of RVs could access, no 4wd needed).…

name: Burr Point Drillhole
description: Great group of sites at the rim of Burr Point, at an old drill hole. There are a couple sites right at this GPS coordinate, as well as a couple farther south along the rim. Fantastic view over the canyon of the Dirty Devil River, and across to Robbers Roost. Great vantage of the…

name: Middle N Cottonwood Creek
description: Excellent, secluded site beside North Cottonwood Creek. Good choice if windy as there is protection from the riverbanks. Cottonwoods for shade and water from nearby North Cottonwood Creek. This area is less busy than farther down the canyon where there is more rock climbing. Loo…

name: Horsethief Bottom S
description: Great campsite right on the Green River at Horsethief Bottom. Shade from a few trees on the river's edge and one can scramble down the bank to jump in the river. Keep a lookout for anthills. Note that portable toilets are required for this area, or just use the pit toilets at th…

name: Gemini Bridges RD
description:
MODERATOR NOTE: Seasonal Closures reported
A great campground in a beautiful canyon and it is free !!! You MUST have a toilet with you.

name: Sunrise country
description:
After rain, location maybe muddy and impassable. For more information, contact land owner Argelia argeliabauer26@gmail.com

This spot is nothing commercial, just a friendly German/Mexican family offering their land. No services available and remote location. Off the grid. The …

name: Mountain Views
description: Beautiful site 30 miles north of Moab. Lots of camping areas. Great tmobile reception.

name: Dispersed near Looking Glass Rd
description: This spot is at the end of a dirt track to the south of Needles Overlook Rd. The road is easily accessible for medium clearance passenger vehicles/vans and camper trailers. RVs I'm unsure, there's a higher grassy middle to the road and a few dips/slanted sections. There's a loop…

name: Dolores River Canyon
description: Road out to site is not bad, a bit bumpy but do-able in 2wd. There is a site above this one suited for RVs or fifth wheels. On the Dolores river, surrounded by canyon walls. There is a fire pit but signs say no wood collecting in area. There are a lot of spots in this area. Ther…

name: Toward radio station
description: Little pull out before the radio station, 1 bar lte, il slept at the radio, juste passing here, enough for a sprinter van and tent

name: Visitor Centre
description:
Potable water by the corrugated building ("tractor annex"), pipe with lever coming out of ground. Pay inside the main building. 
They charge $5.- for water, ask for key inside.

name: BLM disbursed near Canyonlands NP
description: We like this dispersed area although many of the sites were taken. We drove 2 miles and found a good site. Quiet, large, clean, and beautiful. No Verizon.

name: Moab RV & Glaming Resort (formerly OK RV Park)
description: Nice, hot, very clean showers with no time limit. Soap provided. $10 per person/shower

name: Mancos Desert Res
description: BLM spot near a small reservoir with water in the Spring. Remote, quiet, and if there is water you'll get transiting wildlife in this barren land.

name: Riverside
description: Small pull off for 1 van, maybe 2, with amazing view. Right off the dirt road but perfect view of water and canyon.

name: Mineral Springs
description: About 1/2 mile off 313 on Mineral Springs road. Dirt road on the left. Road runs about 3/4 mile loop. Several fire pits and good level spots. 2 bars on ATT and Verizon, which is amazing. Honestly the BLM campgrounds are ridiculously crowded. You can hear a few cars, but you have…

name: Beef Basin Majestic View
description: There are several campsites along Beef Basin Road. This is the first among a group of about two or three coming from the west. This is the largest site and could easily handle a few overlanding rigs and several tents. This site in my opinion has the best view as it is wide open.…

name: Needles Outpost Shower(s)
description: Walk-in showers are $10 per token. You put your token in the box and you get approx 5 minutes of hot water. Note that when I was there only one shower stall, of the two, was working. You do have to buy a token at the entrance station. Don’t know how that impacts hours.

name: Sam's Club
description: Easy parking west side of Sam's club. employees very helpful and courteous, stating no problem parking overnight. Great reception, saw semi trucks in and out the back, so room for larger rigs.

name: Seven Eleven Gaz Station
description: Potable water (we asked) beside the pump #5, the other one is broken. Red spigot.

name: established BLM with insane views
description: the BLM land on the right as you come down Dubinky We'll rd has some amazing views. 4WD or AWD recommended because the dirt road has some boulders and ruts that you need to maneuver around. but once you get down, the views are incredible. pretty good AT&T. you must have a Potty …

name: Beach Oasis
description: Amazing quite spot just off the highway. No Verizon, beautiful soft sand,some trees for shade, possible in 2WD. perfect for a free night of camping.

name: La Sal mountains east side of MT Peale
description: Turn off of La sal pass road. Spotty reception between no bars and 2 bars 4g (AT&T) depending on where you stand or park. A cell booster would probably work wonders. Great low key spot.

name: A Clean & Simple Laundry (free WiFi, too)
description: Just like it’s name - clean and simple. First carpeted laundromat I’ve seen! Restroom, change available, vending machines, a wall of dryers, and various sized washing machines. Parking in front and back. Large rigs can fit in the front but driving through the back would be tight…

name: Near Wind Whistle
description: we headed for Wind whistle campground, but all spots taken or too steep. went 1 mile back and found this great spot. As the one described in looking glass rd. was taken we went a bit further into the land and put our truckcamper next to a tree. Totally quiet and beautiful views

name: 25 Rd
description: several dirt pullouts off OHV trails 5G service but no shade. good spot if you are looking to stay close to town and can handle the heat

name: Behind The Rocks #7
description: It's about a mile in from the pavement on the left. On your way in, stay left at the first fork. Follow signs to Picture Frame Arch. Some parts of the road are bumpy but take it slow. 5G AT&T

name: Klondike Road Campsite
description: One of the few spots on Klondike south with good cell coverage. Two great sheltered spots to get out of the wind. Only drawback is you do get some highway noise.

name: pull-out overlooking the Manti-La Sal National Forest
description:
on the North creek line (between Monticello and Newspaper Rock)
Very large pullout and not to close to the road. Very quiet. 
Isolated and good paved road. 
Place for tents in little bush. 
No services. 
Fresh air at this 14h July.

name: Thompson canyon road
description: Wonderful grove of Cottonwoods along a seasonal creek in Thompson canyon. Private land to west, but nice sized pullout to the east. Essentially on the road but lightly travelled road. Room for larger camper and tents. Really long rugs might stuck out into road. No facilities, bu…

name: Polar mesa road
description: Spot along the polar mesa road. Shade with huge pines. No trafic. 2 bar LTE AT&T

name: North beaver mesa
description: Really stunning view from North beaver mesa on Beaver creek,South beaver mesa and several 3700+ m peaks. You can set you table and chairs right on the edge of the cliff. The spot is totally secluded, and can accommodate a couple of small vehicles. High ground clearance recommend…

name: Moab laundry
description: Nice laundromat. Clean, washroom and free wifi. $4.50 for triple load, $6 for giant load, $0.25 for 4 minutes dryer.

name: Love’s Country Store
description:
There are two love’s gas stations and stores. This is a smaller one across the street. It is a convenient store. We parked behind it! 

We asked an employee and he told us to park anywhere on the gravel and we’d be fine! Restrooms were very clean. Friendly staff. 

We are he…

name: Matrimony Spring
description: Free flowing water from a spring. Look at the left leaning crack in the wall; it’s at the bottom on the left where you pull off.

name: near to the Park and baseball area
description: petit parking face au terrain de base ball très calme, il y a quelques maisons autour mais rien de gênant à s installer ici, très calme avec vrai toilette, eau et WiFi, rien a voir avec l autre emplacement pas terrible proposé a green river ...

name: Hart's road
description: Great views in all directions. Some protection from wind. Somewhat level. Very quiet. One bar on Verizon.

name: The Needles
description: Single primitive spot next to The Needles formation. Site has a firepit and 2 bars of cell service. Site is shaded until ~2pm which is nice. Outside of the 20 mile radius from Moab where BLM camping restrictions apply, but still within the zone where "human waste carryout is req…

name: Shady acres gas and grocery
description: Best free Wi-Fi in town. I managed to upload a YouTube video quickly.

name: blm land
description: no camping in first half a mile. camping is allowd beond. great place, no neighbors. no service. close to canyonlands. road up to this place is rutted but doable in dry weather, even with 2wd van and trailer. do not go any further unless 4wd and high clearance.

name: Maverick
description: Free water spigot next to the gas pumps (between pumps #7 and #8). Asked inside and they told me it's for filling up and potable.

name: Creekside canyon beauty
description: This is on BLM land in the Manti-LaSal National Forest, right next to a cattle gate. It is a pull off of John Brown Rd and it being right next to the road is the only downside (although there’s not much traffic here on a Monday afternoon in early October). There is a nice stand …

name: Loves Travel Stop
description: They have propane, a dump station, showers, and laundry. They allow overnight RV parking and you don't have to park with the trucks.

name: Visitor Center
description: There’s a spigot at the left of the visitor center where one can fill up a jug, also a water fill-up station for bottles inside.

name: farther down the road
description:
farther down the road. 21' trailer. easy access. great views. 3 bars AT&T
No one else around. 9.5 miles out 211 just before Bears Ears sign turn right. 2.9 miles and there is a good spot on right. another good spot about quarter of mile on left. Only saw 1 car go by in 3 days.

name: D&B laundromat
description: decent. some machines out of order. There is a nicer laundromat in Blanding, but this one was fine.

name: Chevron Gas Station
description: Men & Women showers available for $10. Pretty clean but extremely busy during season. Early morning and late evening are best times!

name: Nice camp spot with a firepit
description: To access this spot you need 4wd with decent clearance. Has enough space to accommodate at least four vehicles. Has a firepit.

name: BLM land
description: Large spot and very flat. High clearance recommended. It is best not to attempt when wet. No phone service.

name: Canyonlands Overlook
description: The view from this place is Wonderful! You will have a incredible view on Canyonlands! You can park or put your tent near the cliff. There is also a pit fire and lot of dead woods on the ground around. The track to come here is ONLY for 4wd with high clearance! There are big roc…

name: Flat Iron Point
description:
Nice place to camp near the amazing hills. The track is ok for all small car / van according to me. There is a pit fire on site. I advise to hike on the close hills and try to go on top of Flat Iron Point. The landscapes are wonderful! 
In bonus there is ATT signal.

name: Bartlett Washington Rd
description: Beautiful! Amazing views! We made it in our 23 ft sprinter with high clearance and meaty tires-a must once you turn right off old dead horse rd. More sites further if you have 4x4. No cell. No one else on site!

name: Blm near canyonland
description: Not a bad spot at all once you get to it. It’s a two track back but not horrible there’s a few spots you have to go off the trail to another to get around some rough spots unless you got 4x4 then you can get right through most of this and we’ll past where I made it. Spot is on y…

name: Maverick
description: I filled up water jugs from a hose on north side of station. Outside tap on north side of the building, look low closer to the front side of the building. Employee said it was potable water, slight chlorine smell but otherwise ok!

name: Valley view
description: Such an amazing spot. Room for a big truck or SUV. Nice and flat for RTT action.

name: Pack creek rd
description: Pullout along pack creek rd before the pack creek ranch cabins. there are a handful of pullouts along this road and geyer pass rd but a lot of them have "no camping" signs. this one did not. further down the road is a picnic area which has since been burned by a fire so the bath…

name: Off 10 mile road
description:
Out in cattle grazing land. We were near a fence were many ATVers were using. Good for a night, but pretty exposed to winds. 

1 bar Verizon

name: Ice House America
description: Jug h20 fill with great filters. Cooler drop ice and bag ice cheapest around, great for filling cooler before river trip. To all my fellow van lifers out there... there's a barcode to scan and download their app to get a free 16lb bag of ice! score!

name: Past west of Dirty Devil River bridge
description:
Just west of Dirty Devil River bridge on the north side. Red gravel road leads north 500 feet to a level spot. No 4wd or high clearance needed unless you drive further on the road to another spot next to the canyon. 
Very beautiful 360 views. Not to noisy from Hwy 95. Good spot…

name: Rabbit Valley Overlook
description: Very nice overlook down to Rabbit Valley and Westwater. Many nice spots along this road as well as going left at the intersection. Fire rings at many spots. Road in has been torn up recently by vehicles wallowing in it when wet and muddy. Thus very rutted. When dry there is a si…

name: Free camping outside moab
description: Lots of empty spots all along the road. Lots of people in March but still spaces open. Good proximity to gas station, good service AT&T and Verizon

name: Colonnade Arch Trailhead
description:
About 5 camping sites near the trailhead. High clearance 4WD required - no trailers. Great views from campsites. Can be quite windy. 
Trail to Colonnade Arch is not well marked, sporadic cairns and abandoned jeep trail heading northwards from end of road. Last bit to get a good…

name: Dispersed camping 7 sites
description: This spot is Tucked in next the the rocky wall. Room for 10+ rigs. Beyond this spot is hard to maneuver with a van (any 4x4 should be fine). 4 mi hike into picture frame arch. No digging cat holes- pack everything out. Saw lots of cows roaming. 1-2 bars of LTE with Verizon, not …

name: CitiWash & Clean
description:
Clean laundromat, coin operated machines. Change machine available. 
Medium washer $4.50, large washer $6
Dryer $0.25 for 5 mins
Last load 7:30pm
Open and not too busy on Sunday.

name: Mineral Canyon Road
description: Off Mineral Canyon Road. Area has a few great spots. Previous notes say closed but area was booming all weekend. Super Quiet. Dark sky. Almost no traffic. Super close to Horsethief area MTB trails. Super spot.

name: 18 Road dispersed camping
description: Last grouping of free BLM dispersed campsites along 18 road (sites north and east of here are paid and require reservations. Close to trailhead parking for OHV and MTB trails. Several sandy, fairly close together sites with fire rings, that are more or less level. No amenities, …

name: Huge beautiful BLM space
description: Wow this place was magical. I spent the night here on a Monday. I got here around 5 and a lot of the spaces on the road along the way were filled up with other dispersed campers and trailers, but I went quite a ways down the road and ran into this wonderful huge open lot with gr…

name: BLM Rd 137 Big Mesa Area
description: Nice level site overlooking the valley and mesa, private, with an existing fire ring. Road requires a higher clearance vehicle, but our Sprinter did fine to this point. I would not have traveled beyond this site because of deep sand and the road gets rougher. Can get windy on th…

name: Canyon Rims Recreation Area
description: Quiet spot at the end of yet another BLM dirt road. Several informal campsites with fire rings situated between rock formations. The road is a bit rough, but passable even on 2wd. No cellphone reception, no other amenities, but gorgeous views.

name: Isle of the Sky Overlook
description: Large flat site on Utah State Trust land. Sandy and bumpy dirtroad. Awesome views and great sunsets, very remoted and absolutely quiet. Large firering. Exposed when it’s windy. 4x4 and high clearance absolutely needed. Not for trailers. Can be difficult when wet.

name: Black Dragon Canyon view point
description:
Parking lot with pit toilet. Lots of space. No others overnight but plenty in the morning. Some road noise but not too bad. Great view of the canyon. 

,

name: Secluded near Arches Entrance
description: Two fantastic spots set a little ways back from the entrance. When you come to the entrance of the park from north, turn then right and drive along the fence until you get to the hills, about 1/4 mile. As the road curves to the right, the first spot is on the left. The second sp…

name: Woodside
description: This one is a gem. Less than a mile off the highway, yet a gorgeous panoramic view of tiered cliffs to the East. Easy to get to with 2wd and larger rigs. You'll be fine in whatever you're driving. The pin is at a turnaround on top of a rise, but the area has plenty of more priva…

name: 24 hr Ice & Water
description: currently $.50/gal or $2 for 5 gal. drinking water. download the app for a free bag of ice.

name: Riverside Trail
description: Convenient water fountain for filling water jugs. Free.

name: Near the I70 and Access to Moab
description: Before you enter I70, there is a plain space with solid and gravel, easy with a C 25 Class RV. You hear the road but nothing else. Very good LTE!

name: meadow & canyon views
description: Nice level site with a shade tree & several fire rings. 2wd access & good for solo campers or groups. Occasional OHV group, but overall very scenic and secluded. ~20 minutes outside Gateway. No T-Mobile

name: Trin-Alcove Bend Overlook at end of Three Canyon Road
description: About 5 sites at end of road. Some sites along road also. 4WD high clearance vehicle needed to access. No marked trails but easy enough to walk on the slick rock along canyon rim.

name: End of Paradise.
description:
Views for days.

Drive in was longer than expected (25-30mins), I wasn’t sure we would even stay due to not driving far this morning.

This is one of the best views we have had in last year on the road. Amazing.

We stayed the night.

Your on your own, be prepared to be …

name: BLM off Hart's Draw Rd
description: Maybe a half dozen remote quiet spots. You will need a high clearance or 4x4 to get up here but the rugged remote beauty is worth it.

name: Bridger jack off shoot
description: offshoot off of Bridger jack trail. fire pit and an area big enough for 2-3 cars. 4x4 definitely needed.

name: Near Crescent Junction Rest Area
description: Very good access to many spots, you are given a beautiful sunset and a Milky Way starlit night. It's a typical open desert, but easy to get space far from people. Good T mobile connection.

name: DDR Gaging Station
description: Sandy campsite just S of the ford across the Dirty Devil River. The lower section of Poison Spring Canyon does not have a huge number of sites, but this one is decent. You can camp right on what seems like a road because farther along it is washed out. The DDR is very silty so n…

name: Middle Park/10 Ruins
description: Nice sheltered campsite at the NE end of Middle Park, in Beef Basin. This is also the Trailhead for the short hike to 10 Ruins, and there is another set of ruins just above the campsite. There is another bigger spot to camp just to the south at an old drill hole. This campsite i…

name: BLM near Loma
description: Quiet dispersed camping on BLM land. Coming from I70 and Loma, it is the left hand turn just before the 8 mile marker on 139. This pin is to the first spot, but if you keep going down the dirt road (2WD ok) you'll find a number of other spots and eventually even have a view of t…

name: X4 rd
description: Bigger space up by the road right after the cattle gaurd or this spot about 0.5 mile down dirt X4 rd. Right after crossing La Sal Creek. A bit rough in spots on the way down but doable in a Promaster. No Verizon. You can explore the Cashin Mine site about 3 miles further down (o…

name: Bride Canyon Designated Dispersed Camping
description: At least designated campsites in Bride Canyon. Spots are designated on Gaia GPS using the Gaia Overland Map. We camped in the first designated spot under Bride Canyon Arch. Beautiful spot, especially when the sun came up in the morning. Fire pit, reasonable flare enough for our …

name: Love’s Truck Stop
description:
Showers available at Love’s Truck Stop. Fats and hot. Love’s facilities are usually very nice and clean.

Propane also available.

name: Harts Road ridge camp
description: One spot right off the road, then multiple further in. Only ones here in. Pinyon pine and Utah Juniper for cover bigger than our truck camper. No services but 2 bars Verizon

name: BLM off road area
description:
On road 25 in Junction, drive all the way to the end and it starts a BLM gravel road with many pull offs and open areas to park. Great view, cell service and semi- private. Recommend from a local.

(11/2025) Not bad. I don’t find it feels unsafe. There are a few homeless, but …

name: Fruita BLM
description:
Decent spot. Got here on a Sunday p.m. and it's dead quiet. Roads in are good. Some biting flies but they keep it sporty.
0 trees/shade so bring your own.

name: E Main St - Parking Lot
description: This lot is ok for overnighters. no water, bathroom, or dump station. the gas station nearby isn't a 24 HR station and there is a truck mechanic nearby so this is used as truck turning point as well so park where you won't block their way. Another campervan stayed overnight last…

name: Divide Rd
description: Nice treed spot with fire pit. Room for several smaller rigs. No view. Off rd about 50’. Steep entrance if coming south and second easier entrance 100’ further down.

name: Peters Point Road
description: Super spot avec vu magnifique. LTE, Spot à feu en pierre avec bois sec un peu partout, très tranquille et vue Magnifique à 360.

name: Dewey Spring Powerline
description: Take the dirt road exit, as the road winds up and to the left it levels out and there is a tire track road to the right of the more well worn road. You'll see some wooden power/phone poles further up the hill by the rock outcroppings. Follow the tracks till you get to the round …

name: Quiet Valley View at Lockhard Road
description:
Beautiful quiet valley view over a part of Lockhart Road. some fire rings, even a bench with a table at the end of the cove.
High clearance recommended.

name: Access to Anniversary Arch
description: Access to anniversary arch passing through the Arches National Park boarder. Arch is just a couple miles down the road to the north.

name: Gorgeous site within canyon walls
description: Welcome to Mars. Camp surrounded by red canyon walls with a river close by. Amazing star gazing and sunsets. Canyon walls provide shade until mid morning. We have 4WD high clearance, but could be accessible with 4WD van. No facilities down here. Several other campsites next to R…

name: Canyon Oasis
description: Two larger sites in the trees near the river. Easily room for 4+ vehicles at each site. Good amount of shade. Dirt roads to and into canyon were well maintained. High clearance not needed down here, 2WD questionable due to Sandy roads. Surrounded by beautiful red canyon walls. B…

name: near Cisco
description: nice spot for overnight, far away from the highway and the county road to be quiet, great 360 views. no trees.

name: BLM Land - Crazy views
description:
Huge flat spot with crazy 360 views. 
Head to Mineral Canyon road. You will get to the top and see a huge flat rock spot. 
take a right and follow the spur road to the top. 
go around the tree and head straight down the hill. You will see a big, wide open spot right before yo…

name: Sego ghost town
description: Lovely place next to the road, some trees, a fire pit. It’s located next to the old buildings of the Sego mining town. Very quiet.

name: 21 Road Dispersed Camping
description: Paved road to dispersed camping. These coordinates are for the second pull-off. The ground is level but the roads are pretty rutted. You can drive out further on the dirt roads or stay next to the paved road. Good AT&T and T-mobile reception.

name: Klondike Bluffs Rd. School Trust Land
description: Free camping all along this road as long as you stay at designated camping spots. No toilets. Fire rings. By the highway & train and near the airport but it will be quiet enough for us. Cell reception 3 bars & 5G wifi signal (T-Mobile). Many spots along the road for large RVs. D…

name: Maverick Gas Station
description: There is potable water on the side of the building opposite the RV dump. Silver cover in the wall to the right of the side door, go inside to get a key. Very friendly service, and it’s free. You need your own hose, I backed up in front of the garbage dumps, and my 10 m hose was …

name: Geyser Pass Rd
description: This road is right off La Sal Loop. The loop was paved to the point of the turn off, and Geyser Pass is easy driving. It's graded and bumpy, but I wouldn't think 4x4 would be required. We saw a couple spots on the way up before choosing this one, and I'm sure there are more if y…

name: Beef Basin Road Dispersed
description: Nice flat spot off the side of the road. Room enough for truck camper or tent. Camp ring available. First spot we found open down Beef Basin Road.

name: Dirt Road off Hwy
description:
Didn’t sleep here, but did stop to let the pup outside. 

Nothing special here, good for a quick night. Pull onto the dirt road and there’s a big dirt lot that is on BLM.

I had one bar of T-Mobile. I think I heard people shooting guns nearby (?) so be alert just in case you…

name: BLM Black Ridge area
description: This area is part of the accepted dispersed camping around Moab. Need to go 0.6mile from the road like says the sign. No issue with 2WD. Very nice spot, plenty of spaces and plenty of rigs but quiet unless you are beside someone with an annoying generator. See rules on the websi…

name: Canyon land view
description: Easy dirt road to enter site. Amazing view. Just wow!

name: Hailey's turnout
description: I my opinion this is the best spot before the BLM flat area just above here. perfect 5g on T-Mobile and att. flat spot with a super sweet fire pit. lots of cows, shoveled a bit of *** off the spot. there is a nice sunset to be seen here. coyotes in the area so keep your animals …

name: Arches view
description: Secluded, out of the way level spots just below a little hill with sweeping views over the southern rock formations and Arches in the distance. The hill is the highest feature around and provides 360 view! Fire ring, no services. 4x4 and high clearance required, but mostly ok fr…

name: Spotted Wolf View Area
description: Beautiful view/rest area off I-70 east, west of Green River UT. Super quiet as you’re above I-70. Big rig area is separate, so no idling trucks. Vault toilets and trash cans. 0-1 bar t-mobile

name: Great spot off John Brown Canyon road
description: Great views, fire pits, plenty of room for a few campers.

name: Overlook
description: A view point with no signs saying camping not allowed.

name: Single Site off BLM road 140
description: Nice quiet spot by itself just off the road so there is some traffic of people going to other campsites but it quiets down in the evening. It’s nice site if you have a dog because you don’t have to worry about neighbours.

name: Trevor’s point
description: Super sick camping spot with 270 deg views. Almost completely level. Can be accessed by 2wd. Can be windy sometimes

name: Looking Glass Road
description: Just on the corner of 191 and Looking Glass Road. Big area, we were able to fit our 31' class C no problem, got perfectly level pretty easily. Plenty of room for multiple rigs. I don't see any no camping signs. Noise from 191 but not bad. No cell service, but we had Starlink. Th…

name: Cotter Mine Road Blm
description: one of 10 designated dispersed campsites on Cotter Mine Road. I wouldn't advise going further than this spot and would not recommend coming in at dark. so many places to camp but they are blocked off this is one marked as a campsite. pulling on to the road from the highway is a …

name: Big parking Motel6
description:
In front of Motel 6, there is a big open (parking) spot with a historical marker. At this spot, trucks were sleeping overnight.
It will be noisy but at night there is not much traffic. Train horns can be heard throughout the city.
Laundromat can be used at Motel6, $2 30min, bu…

name: National forest just past Pocupine Rim Campground
description: We had planned to stay at the Porcupine Rim Campground but after we got an hour back to the campground it was closed for construction. We continued down the road another mile or so and found a few pull off areas with fire pits. Beautiful views, relatively flat, surprisingly grea…

name: Mountainside Nook
description: A sweet little site a short ways off the road. Look to be a number of places to stay with easy access branching off of the main dirt road. Fire ring at the site.

name: Manti-LaSal viewpoint
description: Beautiful spot just off FS road. Views for miles! Fire ring, AT&T 1-2 bars. Easy access, could fit a few vans.

name: Wild in Arches Valley
description: Quite Place for 3-4 4x4 vehicles only. Fire possible (better if there is no wind) and best view around.

name: Actual camping spots
description:
Lots of places, some with fire ring. Some are very level and others require leveling blocks. Nice trees, beautiful views, and some wild cows roaming around. 

Our front-wheel drive RAM Promaster made it fine, we took it slow! There were a bunch of people camping in a variety o…

name: the big place
description: A very big place (totally free) with more spaces for camping (or bivouac only). Fire possible and landscapes WOW! ... Mountains, parks and sky. Quiet and reachable by any means.

name: Moonshine canyon 2wd trailhead
description: Level campsite with a fire pit on the road towards moonshine canyon hike. There’s a steep hill shortly after this on the road that needs high clearance 4x4, but this spot is easy to get to and you can always hike from here. Had weak 5G t-mobile

name: Isolated BLM Spot Near Klondike Bluffs
description: Great spot about 0.25 miles off the main road and less than a mile from the Klondike Bluffs MTB trailhead. Beautiful views of the La Sal mountains and far from other campers in the area.

name: Wild Best View (4x4)
description: Between two trees a breathtaking view of the canyons. Flat area for 4x4 vehicles. Quiet area with a beautiful starry sky.

name: Lockhart Rd
description: One of many sites close to the road. There are innumerable OHV roads many of which have campsites. This one is level although sandy which gets whipped up in the wind. You can hear the road but there’s very little traffic. No cell signal.

name: campsites by dino tracks
description:
open spaces with fire rings by stomping ground dinosaur tracks

one bar of ATT

name: Horsethief BLM open sky
description: Drive past Horsethief Campground about a mile and you will start to see roads on your right (Google map even shows them). Most of them have small pull off locations good for a light RV spot or more high clearance for even more spots. Most spots have fire rings.

name: Plateau off of 128
description:
Flat spot big enough for several rigs. Road in is passable with 2wd just take your time. 3 bars VZW. 

Google Fi had 5 bars

name: Oowah Lake
description: Nice site by stream before Oowah Lake Campground.

name: Pull-off on La Sal Loop Rd
description: We stayed one night in our van. pretty level spot for van or smaller camper but no turnaround for larger rigs. other spots had better views but it was getting late and we needed to get our little one to bed. 2 bars Verizon and 3-4 LTE bars T-Mobile. There were conflicting signs …

name: Canyon overlook camping
description: Absolutely stunning views! I made it out here in my Forester but it was a slow, meticulous crawl. The view made it worth it! Had a few other campers back here and they had some better 4x4 high clearance trucks. There are tons of mosquitos so bring the bug spray. It can get reall…

name: Coin Operated Laundromat
description: Plenty top loading washing machines with only two double sized ($5) front loading washing machines. Two triple sized machines but they were out of order. Lots of dryers (25 cents per 7 mins) but the attendant said to put only one load per dryer because they don’t get hot enough.…

name: Dispersed Camping
description: Relatively large, flat, hard packed dirt campsite before Fruita Desert. Spot would turn into slop if wet. Several smaller pull offs prior to this spot. Gravel/paved road in decent condition. Fenced (barbed wire) on both sides. Nice area for your dog to run. Stone fire pit. LOTS …

name: Meadow rimmed by pines
description: Beautiful flat open meadow just off fire trail, great views of escarpment walls above. There's a fire ring but no phone signal. Plenty of room for multiple vehicles.

name: Overlook off of Dome Plateau
description: Beautiful overlook with room to camp. Can fit a few vehicles with views of both sunrise and sunset

name: Nice view with fire pit.
description: A little over a mile off the paved road. Nice view of the valley and the wind turbines. No road noise. Spot is fairly level and has a fire ring. There is a short incline to get into the spot. No problem with our 4x4 Sprinter.

name: Warehouse 2565
description: We asked for permission to stay overnight after a ticketed country music show at the venue. I'd only stay if you ask.

name: Colorado Vistor Center
description: Potable water near the dump station in the parking lot of the Colorado Visitor center. May be certain hours that the water is turned on because it wasn’t working at 6am. There’s also water fountains inside the visitor center (check open hours)

name: Shay Ridge Trail Campsites
description: Super cool area with a lake, Mtn views, pit toilet. Follow signs for Shay Ridge Trail. Several large, flat, private campsites with fire pits. Totally quiet. Not far from Canyonlands. Super pretty! Some cell coverage.

name: BLM Land off highway with a view
description: Just a mile off the highway, follow the dirt road and take left to the rim. A beautiful mostly even spot. Wide open, view ofer the valley and a rim, there is a firepit too

name: Sandstone Formation Views
description: Nice BLM marked site with fire ring. Super cool views. Nice sunset and pretty quiet except others coming in to camp up the road etc. Big enough for our extended E350. Wouldn’t fit a scholie or true RV. 4WD or AWD with decent clearance recommended. I’d do it in my Subie with caut…

name: Canyon lands NP view BLM wild camping
description: Gorgeous view of Canyonlands with a great sunrise! Wild camping lots of rock fire pits 4x4/ AWD needed did it in a stock f150 saw a Subaru here to!

name: Desert tent spot
description:
Perfect spot in the desert with awesome view. Perfect for cyclists. 
Worth pushing a bit further to avoid lights and noise. 
Wonderful place

Food and water nearby, full phone service

name: Lone Mesa BLM dispersed camping
description: Road is good, several spots with fire rings, 2 bars att, most spots fairly level, a bit windy but the view is beautiful

name: BLM
description: Was looking for the other spot sited here but didn’t find it in the dark. Found this spot near by. Big enough to fit a big rig, flat, nice views around, lots of stars and great cell coverage (between 1-3 bars). Great overnith spot near highway 70. Read easily navigable when dry.

name: Copper Mine Ridge Rd
description:
A large campsite on the East Side of the mining hill. 
The mountain helps block the wind if coming from the west and unlike the campsites out in the open, you'll get shade here by about 4:30 each day. Great cell service and easy to get to off the highway. The road can get rutte…

name: Large spot off main road
description: Just one spot off the main road. Big rig friendly it’s just going to be tight for a big rig. 3-4 bars LET Verizon. Quiet and nice escape from Moab heat

name: Lockhart Plateu
description: A little further up the side drive from Lockhart Road site at the top of the hill is a relatively level solid rock area out of the sand and dust with your own Hamburger rock! A couple rocky spots on the driveway up but I did it fine in my small ft wheel drive SUV. No shade till …

name: Windwhistle
description: Open. Has no amenities. Beautiful view of bluffs.

name: Moab Rim RV Campark
description:
Showers available for non-guests, $5 per person.

Hot water, good pressure, clean shower, no time limit. Toilets, sinks, hand soap in the same room for use as well. Pay in the office, then go to the shower/restroom around the side of the building.

3 shower stalls for men, 3…

name: in the middle of wild wild west
description: if you conquered the Hurray Pass and checked out the natural caves this is a good spot the camp next to the fire ring with walkable access to the river

name: Marked camp spot
description: Marked camp spot says up to 14 days. Right next to river can see bridge

name: Rabbit Valley Overlook
description:
One of the many designated camp sites in Westgate. This site overlooks Rabbit Valley and has amenities!

There's two pit toilets (one brand new one for 2025) and picnic tables at the overlook. Plenty of shrubs to help break some of the wind.

The final quarter mile to this o…

name: Causeway Sth of Wilson arches in Moab
description: Pretty poor road from the north or south entrance but all doable in any vehicle if you take it steady. We have a 5th wheel and no trouble. Pulled up next to the causeway and saw no one in 2 days. Small amount of highway noise but perched on top of a canyon, this would be one of …

name: Overlook
description: Verizon 2 bars, three vehicles went by while I was here. Good road is easy for two wheel drive. Clean and beautiful

name: South Town Gym
description:
$20/Day Pass. Provides full access to cardio and gym equipment, dance room, tanning, sauna (out of order) and showers.

Middle shower (Men’s Locker Room) has very good water pressure. Worth a stop if you’re looking for a workout and a place to clean up. 24/7 facility.

name: Little Flat Top / Lower San Rafael Road
description: Great spot nestled at the base of Little Flat Top with really cool rock formations scattered about. Close to a road that can be driven fast but only had about 2-3 vehicles pass in morning and maybe one in evening. Very peaceful. Bonus: verizon signal good at lower spot, seemed a…

name: Veteran's Memorial Park
description: City Park with playground, skate park, tables, bathrooms. No signs indicating that overnight parking is not allowed. There is angle parking for smaller vehicles and parallel parking along street for larger units.

name: Not too far after Horsethief campground
description: Drive past Horsethief Campground about a mile. It’s right before the other spot noted on here as “Horsethief BLM open sky”

name: Westerner RV park
description: $10 cash only private showers with toilet and sink too. Water is very hot and good pressure. Nothing fancy but the place is clean. Pay at the office.

name: Old Mining Cave
description: Lots of dispersed spots in this aream plenty of open sky for solar and Starlink. Cell is so so on ATT. Old mining cave near by. Road in can get a bit rough from at and ohv traffic. Clean and quiet.

name: Big Tire Campsite
description: Great site for extended stay. Mostly gravel so good in rain. Lots of room for any size rig to turn around and get level. Fire ring looks old but is usable. South approximately 8.25 miles from I-70. Looks for big heavy equipment tire on right. Campsite is immediately past big tir…

name: Black Dragon Canyon
description: Stayed in our truck camper at the base of the canyon. Decent ATT signal. great hiking and biking up in canyon! Make sure to be aware of weather. Roads in/out could be a real problem in wet weather. Some clearance is adviced. Roads are worn out and uneven. 4WD is optional. Depend…

name: Elrics duck off
description: Down BLM road 107 off route 128. 4x4 is not needed unless roads are wet. No one around the whole way back here. It's big enough for 4-5 rigs with still room to hang out. No cell service.

name: Side road past horsethief campground
description: Drive past horsethief campground, and after a little while there is an unmarked dirt road (just a sign that says "dead end") on the left. There are spots all along this road, mostly fairly spread apart. Road was a little rutted but not too bad, not sure if it gets worse further …

name: Naturita Town Park
description: Dark paved lot right by the river. Plenty of spots. 3 bars of version. No camping sign but no sign prohibiting overnight parking. Toilets up the road. Where closed when I went but had a port-a potty outside. Visited November 2024

name: Valley city rd
