description: BLM land free. Verizon 2 bars. Mid November 20 degrees over night. 40 day.

name: La Sal Range View
description: Pull out from road that goes back just a little ways with plenty of spots for several rigs. Several flat spots and an established rock fire pit. Very exposed to the winds. Great views of the La Sal Mountain Range just outside of the Arches N.P. boundary. Road at this point is we…

name: Best Site
description: 5g, enough room for a couple large rigs, multiple fire pits, good distance from main road

name: Looking toward arches
description: Red rock views. Can bike/moto or walk to the border of Arches NP from this spot easily. Muddy when wet. When dry- Nice for bike riding.

name: Windy Point
description: BLM free camping. Fire ring and flat areas to pitch a tent. Lots of space for a van or camper. Might need 4x4 up the road. It was windy when we were here but beautiful. We had ATT and Verizon as strong service.

name: Hidden Petroglyphs
description: Free BLM camping with a fire ring. At the end of the road there are vault toilets. No 4x4 necessary. If you face NE from the fire ring you can see a large boulder with some petroglyphs. they are noticable by their triangle shape humanoid figures. Couple other spots near by that …

name: Triangle road BLM 107
description: BLM 14 days. Large open space, easy road. Fire pits. Probably muddy when raining. No cell signal.

name: Lone Mesa Dispersed Camping
description: Making a new post under wild camping. Good area with quite a few flat areas. No fee camping only in designated spots, about a dozen here + an acre of common area near one of the fire pits. Must have a portable toilet to camp in all Lone Mesa

name: Hooks & Ladders ohv
description: Hooks and ladders trailhead with a few campsites established. Cool spot to ride and camp for free. Spotty cell service but works, great starlink.

name: Big Indian Road
description: We were looking for a place to spend the night south of Moab. This spot is flat and easy on/off the road. The old highway is just behind the motorhome, is paved, and would be a good place to back in a trailer if your rig is a bigger rig. There is some road noise since it’s close…

name: Outside Canyonlands NP
description: Drive over 1/2 mile off road and you’re good to go! So peaceful! Sandy road in, no off road vehicle required. Didn’t see another soul. Level.

name: Moab BLM
description: Large flat lot just off the highway. Dirt road in, muddy and rutted if recently rained. Enough space for many rigs. We parked here overnight on our way to Arches, and there were 5 other vans/RVs in the lot. Peaceful. Perfect cell service for T-Mobile. 2 bars for AT&T. 3 bars Ver…

name: Large flat area
description: A little further down Peters road. Turn left at the fork. Room for a couple of rigs. Road has a couple of big ruts but should be navigable for most back country friendly units. We pulled a 30 ft Class C in an F550 chassis no problem.

name: Goblin Valley Viewing area
description: lots of parking for small and large vehicles or RVs. Amazing viewing area, pit toilets, and very close to interstate. didn't see any signs forbidding overnight parking but please double check.

name: Looking glass pasture
description: Cow pasture with watering trough. Had 2 bars verizon cell and AT&T. Beautiful sites surround the area.

name: NEEDLES OVERLOOK
description: Need 4x4 to get into the site. It's not that level, but both At&t and verizon have great coverage. Speed test shows 9mbps. Looks towards the southwest, so it's great to watch sunsets!

name: blm
description: 14 days stay blm near canyons, road a little bumpy but we made it with our 2wd high clearance

name: Clearing in the woods
description: This is a clearing in at the foot of a mountain room for several vans or cars there’s even room for pull behind camper looks like it’s used for hunting maybe

name: Near Radio Towers
description: Great campsite with a fire ring just behind camp you have commanding views of the valley, access is with high clearance vehicle or four-wheel-drive, trailer is not advised established fire ring room for one maybe two vehicles mostly flat. Two bars, AT&T LTE network.

name: BLM
description: (Free) Stayed here a night and I was the only person for miles. Drive past Papa Joe’s, over the train tracks, turn left on the dirt road camp site is on the left with a fire pit. Was able to connect to data via AT&T

name: Public park
description: Park bathrooms and water. Big soccer and playground. Lots of kids. Keep it clean

name: Old Quarry
description: Large flat area with plenty of space for several big rigs.

name: CMU Maverick Center
description: CMUs rec center. $8 for a day pass. Free Guest WiFi, nice pool with tons of lanes (closed from 2-7pm for team practice), workout area, and nice showers with hot water.

name: Valley City Rd, BLM 144 (0.6mi)
description: Dispersed camping area along Valley City rd. There are many spots with flat(ish) areas and plenty of room to space.out between campers. We stayed here over Easter weekend and it wasn't too busy. Quite, with a little highway noise. Beautiful sunrise sunset, but no trees. Starlink…

name: BLM - borrow pit Mineral Bottom Rd
description: BLM land. Variable little (1 bar) to no Verizon cell service. Access on left side coming from paved road. Looks like a borrow pit. No fire rings. Wide open space relatively accessible for bigger rigs (more than previous turnoffs we passed) and even for some to turn around. Gun s…

name: Sands Flat Rd 2mi off of Loop rf
description:
3 bars Verizon. Easy couple of pickups of small to mid trailer. 
Fairly flat and great view.

name: Cabela’s and Bass Pro
description: Cabelas and Bass Pro store allows overnight parking (I asked inside). They request that you park near the restaurant(s) side of the parking lot. They warned not to park outside the side boundaries of the store (so just in front of the store and not in front of other stores (it’s…

name: Spot for One
description: Easy camp for one possibly two campers. Road isn’t too bad. There are a lot of options around here.

name: Sand flats big spot
description: Has cell service several fire rings, fits multiple vehicles.

name: BLM land
description: Great spot with lots of wild camping areas along the way. Great views all around. Full bars with T-Mobile and two bars with AT&T.

name: Marlboro Point
description: Site just before Marlboro Point. 4WD high clearance necessary. Parked right on edge of cliff overlooking canyon lands. ATT 2 bars. Beautiful site.

name: Rocky Alcove
description: Easy dirt drive. Big parking area. Slightly sloped. Rock fire ring. 2-3 bars of ATT 5G or LTE. Nice and tucked in location. Quiet open views to the SW

name: Lockhart rd
description:
Very nice campsite up a slight hill and off the main road. Red rock formation on three sides give some late day shade and morning sun. 
Fairly level parking for truck, van or small trailer. No cell service.

name: By Green River. 4x4 only
description: No cel service. Sporadically along the way, but not here.

name: Canyons and mountains at a distance
description: Nice place to spend the night. Coming from the south there’s a lot of soft sand. Don’t know from the North yet. 4x4 only. Good cel signal (3 bars LTE).

name: Bartlett Wash
description: Large space at the beginning of Bartlett Wash Rd on the right side. Hard packed (went through a thunderstorm while here and didn’t get very muddy), large space and has a fire ring. Dirt road in wasn’t bad at all either…easily 2WD or bigger trailer friendly. 2 bars 5G service wit…

name: Trick Stop Pilot - Sinclair Gas Station
description: Trucker stop, so expect noise all night long, but still an option with bathroom and shower open 24h.

name: Pilot Sinclair Truck Stop
description: Private toilet and Hot showers for $17. No time limit.

name: Floy Cyn road
description: Good cell signal. Don’t need 4x4 but limited to approximately 20ft vehicle because of deep wash crossing

name: The lonesome spot
description: Great large secluded wild camping spot, cellphone service worked great, lagged sometime but then went back to 5g. Beautiful view of the snowy mountains and canyons. If it rains and ground is soft would NOT try it, otherwise saw sedans parked in a nearby camp. A big rig can fit a…

name: Floyd Canyon Loop Camp
description:
*Edit: can't access if it's just rained, or without a high clearance 4x4 and a lot of determination.*

This site is off the highway and just far enough away from the train that you don't hear much. Cell coverage is pretty good, and the views aren't bad either.

This trail is…

name: Salt Valley Road Pull Off
description: Back off the road far enough you won't get dusted out from passing vehicles. Quiet and mostly secluded. 4 bars verizon 4G.

name: Ridge Line
description:
BLM 14 day. Drove up in my 31’ Class C towing a Jeep. Go slow. Easy access. Lots of campfire rings (sadly no fires allowed). AT&T 5G 3 bars. This site is tight and uneven but the rest are big and flat. 
Beautiful view

name: Canyon overlook
description:
Signal 3 bars on t mobile but slow internet, suitable for big rigs could accommodate a 40'
In 20 states this is the nicest place I've found.

name: BLM Mineral Canyon Rd
description: Nice spot with a little shade in the afternoon! Decent sized spot, though with a larger rig it might not be level. Here in a 2wd van, looks like there might be spots farther down the road too. Spotty verizon service, a little highway noise.

name: BLM high ground, exit 175
description:
Tread lightly. used less than other spots just down the hill, but I preferred this higher ground for my smaller 2WD van.
excellent Verizon signal .

name: Woodsy campsite
description: Nice little spot in the trees with a fire ring. A little bit of service and Starlink actually works well here. Maybe one more spot up the road, but less than a mile up the road there’s a no camping sign due to it being in the watershed. This spot seems allowed though!

name: La Sal view
description: Easy to access spot right off La Sal loop road. Dirt road with bumps but no need for high clearance or 4x4. Bring levelers if you’re not in a tent but super quiet spot. Only ones here and one car drove further down the dirt road. Great cell signal too. Epic sunset.

name: Lost Springs Wash OHV Parking
description: Very large flat parking area. Cell service is excellent. Train tracks across highway so kind of noisy but if you’re tired and not ready to drive further it’s a good place to stop. Lots of OHV trails nearby. We didn’t see any no camping signs. BLM land.

name: Behind the Rocks
description: single dirt road that leads to camp site and fire pit; 2 bars cell service (AT&T); open views of the mesas/red rocks; can take motorbike, e-bike or bikes to trailheads to hike; we hiked to Moab Rim Arch and Tukuhnikivatz Arches; quiet in evening; some ATVs/Jeeps in day passing b…

name: Railroad Pulloff
description: 5G signal - close to the road so some definite noise, but read was rocky enough that I didn’t want to risk going further in my 2WD since it looks like there may be rain coming. Not the flattest spot but will work for a night

name: North Creek
description: The road in is rough but we managed in a Subaru. Larger RVs won't fit but a teardrop could probably make it. Spotty service, one bar in and out for AT&T and Verizon. It's quite close to the road so you can hear traffic. Lots of deer like to hang out in the area so be mindful of …

name: Lovés gas station
description: Water spigot at pump nº14. Non potable water but ok to fill out the RV water tank.

name: Another amazing spot
description: Another amazing spot wild camping. Road not bad coming in as long as you take your time not recommended in a car. There were other campers in other near by spots but didn’t hear them. No cell service for us but great starlink. Carry out all your trash leave no trace

name: BLM great view
description: Needs 4WD to access. A local friend gave me these coordinates. About 40min down BLM dirt road, wonderful views across valley - snow capped mountains in distance. Fire pit present but we didn’t light a fire as warnings in place.

name: Mineral Bottom Overlook
description:
Mineral Bottom Overlook 
Alone our favorite

name: Labyrinth Canyon Overlook
description: Green River Labyrinth Canyon Overlook alone

name: Juste another camp are in the BLM
description: Quiet place for a night or mire in the BLM. Steeple dire road to access it. Probablement notre doable with all RV in wet conditions.

name: Just another camping place
description: Another spot in the BLM. Nice viewpoint abs quiet. Access possible with big rig in dry conditions

name: BLM road truck spot
description: 5 g available, smaller vehicle only, 4x4 preferred

name: Shell Gas Station
description: Red faucet outside near propane tank. Potable water according to staff and free to fill up. Good water pressure.

name: First Place from Mill Canyon Dino Tracks
description: Fairly big spot with fire ring, couple of trees and views of a gorgeous rock wall and the valley. Camping permitted for up to 14 days. Good phone signal. I came in from the Mill Canyon Dinosaur Tracksite, and the road was pretty rough in a couple of spots (steep inclines with de…

name: Mineral Bottom Rd- Dispersed Camping
description: Acouple of different spots along this road. This one is a decent size loop, I got here in my 2wd SUV with AT tires on it with no problem. Nice view of the sunset, not far off the road but very quiet and still private

name: Behind The Rocks #6
description: Campsite behind The Rocks #6 Reachable with a 2WD van. Just go slow and chose a good path then its totally doable. In our opinion no high clearance needed because not washed out that big. Also its not that Steep & not a lot of loose sand. Most of the time Rocky with a lot of tra…

name: Gemini Bridge Road
description: Nice, large place just off the Gemini Bridge Road. Weak AT&T service. Very quiet day and night.

name: Off the road
description: I needed a place after a heavy rain storm. This spot is just a pull off from a gravel road. No cars went by in the time I was here. I had great cell service and good view

name: Canyon views BLM
description: Near green river, road was fine for 2wd beautiful views and cool spots to explore good service lte 4 bars on top of hill, large pull off side of road could fit several vehicles with a campfire ring

name: Mineral Point Road Vista
description: Great BLM dispersed site on Mineral Point Road. Fantastic view over the Canyon Land park, all the way back toward Moab. Easy dirt road when it’s dry, evidence of surface water after last week’s rain. No services, no people, no noise. Fabulous up here.

name: A view
description: Pretty private, one dirt bike has gone by. 3 bars verizon

name: Opposite of Kane Creek Canyon OHV
description:
Easily got my 26' rig here. 
There aren't many places for large rigs, and if you're pulling a long trailer, you'd tear up the area turning around, so don't.
Will be able to level without much effort.
There is a cell tower just barely visible above here, but even with a booste…

name: Salt Valley Road
description: Flat ground for tent or car/camping. Fire Ring. Close to a Family-Climbing-spot. Not much for shade. Quiet! Nice rocks

name: Cottonwood road
description: Just off the freeway a good spot to pull over and park for the night. Fits my 60’ pull behind. Enough space to turn around as well. Slow signal

name: Maverik
description: Between pump 19 and 20. Guy on the gas station told us is potable water for filling

name: Spring Canyon Point
description:
Beautiful spot at the rim just a 1/2mile before the big turnaround at Spring Canyon Point. Better view then at the turnaround.
Average cell service.
The road is not bad but Big Ricks could feel more comfortable with hig clearance at some rocky parts.

name: BLM Land near Canyonlands NP
description: Nice site tucked in among some junipers. Very weak AT&T signal. No Verizon. Rutted and sandy double track road to get back to it.

name: Utah Welcome Center Rest Stop
description: Spigot coming out of the ground to the left behind the 'Welcome to Utah' sign, right along concrete walking path from the car-parking area. Sign at spigot describes how to use pump but does not indicate whether it's potable or not. Spigot is walk-up only, can't drive up to it. N…

name: Loop
description: a short loop with room for four vehicles and a couple fire rings.

name: Wildcamp Utah
description: Beautiful location. Quiet and with a beautiful view of Arches National Park.

name: Civic Center parking lot
description: Overnight parking allowed including for oversized vehicles. Very peaceful, dark in the areas away from the overhead parking lot lights. I had plenty of cell service and was able to get a level parking spot for my camper van. Portable toilets on the property but were locked. The …

name: BLM
description: Other multi vehicle sites nearby but far enough for privacy. I came after some rain making the road a bit tricky but I made it in a crosstrek with no problems. Decent T-Mobile service, it went sos if I stood in the wrong spot too long.

name: Gravel pulloff near I-70
description: Big gravel pull off near I-70. After you exit for Mack, turn right towards the town of Mack and it should be on your right almost immediately. 4 LTE bars for Verizon. Barely hear the highway. Could fit multiple cars/vans and 1-2 big rigs.

name: Down Floy Canyon Rd
description: Nice spot with views at the end of a trail. The main road is currently blocked at this point due to a wash out. 3 bars of 5G with AT&T.

name: Pullout
description: A small pullout off a gravel road suitable for small class a.

name: Gear heads outdoor store
description: Outdoor store that has free water refills around the back. Can also buy water carriers, butane etc

name: BLM pullout
description: Another road onto BLM land. Close gate behind you. Loop about 100 yards down the road. Road continues farther if you want to get out of sight of the highway.

name: BLM camp
description: Just past the cattle guard on the right side of the road if you are coming from the west. Large pull through spot that can easily fit several smaller rigs or a big one. I didn't have service here but I could get it within walking distance.

name: BLM near Moab
description: Many sites available in borg sides of the road. 14days max, no wood gathering. Firering Provided

name: Owl Draw View
description: Nice flat area with view of LaSal Mountains. Perfect for a night, quiet. Road in was in good condition, gets a bit steep at the end but no issue for our 2wd van in dry conditions (we first checked out Owl Draw Road which was worse and no view). Good cell signal. Enjoy and rememb…

name: Pull off on old Cisco Hwy
description: Small pull off on old Cisco hwy just of I70. Good for a night. Quiet, hardly any traffic overnight. Plenty of space for a few vans or smaller rigs. 3 bars of cell signal, no amenities.

name: Colorado river overlook
description:
Big open space and relatively flat with great views around and into the canyon below. Opposite of the Thelma and Loise point on the other side of the river.

Last spot before the end of the Chicken Corners Trail. You will need a high clearance 4x4 to get here. No cell service.

name: BLM Peace
description: Three bars of 5G a bit of a tricky hill to pull up on. I made it up in my 99 Chevy express 3500 with 2 wheel drive. Still would be tricky though in anything without all terrain tires. BLM land so should be fine.

name: Fruita Welcome Center
description: Free drinkable water at RV dump station. Two spigots available. Slow water pressure.

name: Overflow area for rest stop
description:
Large dirt lot across from rest stop. It appears to be an overflow lot as it was used by two semi’s in the night. Can handle larger vehicles.

It still has the road noise but doesn’t have as much noise from idling trucks as the rest stop does. Be sure to park on the outside of…

name: Next to Super 8 parking lot
description: There’s a flat dirt pullout just past the Super 8 hotel parking lot. Stayed from 9pm-8am it was nice and quiet, no cars really driving by and no one bothered us. Some cellphone signal. You’ll need to go to the gas station to use the restrooms. Took a shower at the Love’s Travel …

name: South of the airport
description: Great easy spot off the highway, just south of the airport. Huge area/field to camp in. Any size rig, as long as its not wet. Gravel road, full service. Talked to a sheriff at arches today, told us this whole road is available for camping. Great for star watching.

name: Jeffery Wells Rd
description:
Road just W of Little Flat. Easy access for ONE car/ van. 1 spot alongside road about 1/4 mile up. Flat. Great views. No wind this time but would be windy.
1/4 further up the road is larger flat spot - room for 2-3 cars/ vans.

name: Lockheart road cattle crossing
description: Great spot, level, easy road access for 2wd. Fire pit. Cattle all around us. Minutes from canyonlands np. Zero cell service.

name: Mineral bottom camping
description: Not that there needs another wild camping out here, this place is super easy to camp at. The “road” has a huge turnaround for group camping. We’re tucked in before hand, super quaint. Great sunset as always. 2wd works. No high clearance needed. 1 bar cell

name: The Confluence
description:
Pull out at the intersection of two roads with a small berm that offers a level spot if you park it right. 
Mostly quiet with a few cars going by.
3 bars 5G T-Mobile outside if the wind is right.

name: Nefertiti
description: This is the put in for the green river daily float. There are two car turn around areas where you can park and two vault toilets. There are areas where you can camp on the beach near the river through the trees from parking area. Make sure to leave space for vehicles with traile…

name: Speedy Laundromat
description:
Clean, modern machines. Accepts credit cards or coins. Coin exchange machine and laundry supplies on site. They have 10 washers and 12 dryers. Open 24 hours. WiFi available .
Caution! Google Maps will not bring you to the laundromat. Turn at the U-Haul business off of 191 and l…

name: Hell Roaring canyon overlook
description: Amazing spot with views of hell Roaring canyon, surrounding formations. Very large area. Other sites in the area. Opportunity to hike into hell Roaring canyon from campsite. No cell service. Accessible with Toyota Corolla Cross.

name: Geimi Rd spot
description: This is a nice spot stayed one night here. Lots of spots along this road. Verizon LTE 2 bars!

name: Potash Road
description: Occasional vehicles drive by, but super quiet otherwise. There are pullouts of various sizes along the road plus a couple of established campgrounds that looked to be free or at least minimal cost. We spent one night at a pullout by the Colorado River. Stunning spot! No internet…

name: BLM - Views
description:
*Edit: Can't access as of April 2026 without a high clearance 4x4 and a lot of determination.*

Nice flat spot on right. Great rocks and views. 1 bar ATT. Easy graded road. Some sand, but not a big deal. BLM land. No signage about camping anywhere since I-70.

name: Flat spots with a view
description: A bit of a rough road with a few spots that got washed out, but the road is solid and short. Three spots on the ridge that look like camping spots or just junk drops. Quite lovely though, and a little privacy from the main road.

name: Mesa on Lower San Rafael Rd.
description: Beautiful view from a short side road at 16 miles out the Lower San Rafael Rd towards Horseshoe Canyon. Decent cell phone.

name: Arches
description: Beautiful place with a great view of the arches.

name: Wonderful cottonwood tree and pull out campsite
description: Nice cottonwood tree for shade to camp while visiting Sligo.

name: In the bush
description: I decided to stop here because the road is very bumpy and requires a high level of clearance. As I arrived at sunset, I didn’t want to risk driving in low light conditions. There isn't an amazing view, but there is a fireplace and it's flat enough. It's also the closest to the e…

name: Mountain overlook
description: Limited cell phone, no satellite availability.

name: Cow Pie Cave
description: Easy road to get here. Got here in a 2 wheel drive Sprinter easy. Very cool unless you don’t like cow poop. If you don’t mind cow poop you’ll love it. Maybe bring a rake?

name: Sunrise Vista
description: Nice view and sunrise over the hills. Made it without 4x4

name: Old Cisco Rd turnout
description: A dirt turnout along Old Cisco rd. Like many of the turnouts on the road, it is dirt, and could be an issue if muddy. Room for 1 big rig. The turnout is like a hump (or mound). I was able to circle on my way in, and level the rig on the mound. This is on the edge of BLM, actuall…
